paper_analysis.summary: This paper demonstrates a hard induced superconducting gap in high-mobility planar Ge/SiGe quantum wells by forming buried PtSiGe superconducting contacts through a thermally activated solid-phase reaction. Using low-temperature transport in Josephson junctions, quantum point contacts, SQUIDs, and a 2D island array, the authors show near-unity interface transparency, multiple Andreev reflections, a hard gap with strong subgap suppression, phase control, and scalable superconducting behavior in germanium.
measurement_types: fast Fourier transform analysis of STEM images

figures.description: Schematic of the fabrication flow for a superconductor-normal-superconductor quantum point contact: Pt deposition, thermal anneal to form PtSiGe, then gate deposition with dielectric insulation.
Cross-sectional HAADF-STEM image of the SNS-QPC showing the Ge quantum well, SiGe barriers, PtSiGe contacts, and gate layers.
Atomic-resolution HAADF-STEM image of the Ge/PtSiGe interface with FFTs and a unit-cell schematic for the orthorhombic PtSiGe phase.
EELS composition maps for Pt, Ge, Si, Al, and O across the device cross-section.
SEM top-view image of the SNS-QPC device with PtSiGe contacts and gate layout; channel dimensions are annotated in the text.
Color map of voltage drop versus source-drain current and constriction-gate voltage, plus normal-state conductance trace versus gate voltage.
Differential conductance versus source-drain bias and constriction-gate voltage showing multiple Andreev reflections; line cuts are fit with a coherent scattering model to extract transparency.
Conductance maps versus temperature and in-plane magnetic field, showing closing of the induced gap and critical temperature/field behavior.
SEM image of the NS-QPC device with one superconducting PtSiGe lead and one normal Ge 2DHG lead.
Conductance versus bias and constriction-gate voltage in the NS-QPC, with line cuts showing the hard induced gap and gate dependence.
Conductance versus in-plane magnetic field and gate voltage, with line cuts showing gap robustness up to finite field.
Normalized conductance traces for six NS-QPC devices in tunneling regime, demonstrating reproducible hard-gap behavior across devices.
SEM image and schematic of a two-junction SQUID with independently tunable Josephson junctions.
figures.likely_source: experimental data for image, conceptual schematic for overlay

figures.description: Voltage drop across the SQUID versus current and out-of-plane magnetic field, showing switching-current oscillations and phase control.
3D and top-view schematics of a 51 × 10 PtSiGe island array, plus AFM image of the islands.
figures.likely_source: experimental data for AFM, conceptual illustration for schematic

figures.description: Sheet resistance versus current and gate voltage, and versus current and out-of-plane magnetic field, showing global switching and flux commensurability effects in the array.
Sheet resistance versus temperature for several gate voltages, showing insulating-to-superconducting evolution.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements from low-frequency four-terminal transport on SNS-QPC, NS-QPC, SQUID, and 2D array devices, including current-bias and voltage-bias traces, conductance maps, temperature sweeps, and magnetic-field sweeps. It also reports raw microscopy measurements from HAADF-STEM, EELS elemental mapping, and AFM imaging of the fabricated heterostructures and devices.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: All data included in this work are openly available at the 4TU research data repository (https://doi.org/10.4121/19940174). All other relevant data are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are not described in a file-format-specific way, but the paper indicates that the underlying experimental data supporting the figures are deposited in the 4TU repository. The raw measurements come from transport experiments in dilution refrigerators and from microscopy instruments (HAADF-STEM, EELS, AFM).
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include normal-state conductance traces, normalized conductance curves, extracted transparency values from coherent-scattering fits, induced gap values from MAR and coherence-peak fits, critical temperatures and critical fields, and fitted SQUID parameters such as effective loop area and self-inductance. The paper also reports EELS-derived stoichiometries and compositional profiles.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly implied: the paper states that conductance maps for all six NS-QPC devices are shown in Supplementary Fig. 9, device geometries in Supplementary Fig. 8, and additional supercurrent discretization, Fraunhofer patterns, and array measurements in supplementary figures. The main text figures are based on experimental source data and fitted overlays, but the paper does not explicitly state whether per-figure source-data files are separately bundled.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned as being provided. The Methods state that MAR simulations were implemented in Python using a modified version of code from a cited reference, but the script itself is not described as part of the dataset.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset appears to combine multiple modalities: transport traces/maps, microscopy images, elemental maps, and fitted/simulated curves. The paper emphasizes reproducibility across six NS-QPC devices and includes supplementary figure data for additional devices and measurements. The repository likely contains figure-supporting data rather than only raw instrument outputs, but the paper does not specify file organization, formats, or whether source-data files are separated from raw measurements.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper explicitly says all data are openly available in the 4TU research data repository and describes direct experimental measurements from transport, STEM, EELS, and AFM.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper reports figure-specific extracted quantities such as transparency from fits, induced gap values, critical fields, SQUID fit parameters, and supplementary figure data for multiple devices, indicating processed/source data accompanying the figures.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “All data included in this work are openly available at 4TU research data repository https://doi.org/10.4121/19940174.”; “The simulations of conductance were implemented in Python using a modified version of the code presented in ref. 68.”; “Supplementary Fig. 8”; “Supplementary Fig. 9”; “Supplementary Fig. 10”; “Supplementary Fig. 11”.
